skill.name: music-generation
skill.description: Generate instrumental music as MP3 or WAV files using music21, MIDI tools, FluidSynth, and electronic synthesis helpers.
skill.summary: Create and render original instrumental music from structured composition prompts.
skill.author: Cam10001110101

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported critical combinations, but review found no prompt injection, credential handling, or network exfiltration. Most high-severity hits are false positives from music terms, private skill paths, documentation URLs, and local rendering examples. The remaining real risks are legitimate installer commands and local file operations, so publication is acceptable with warnings.

medium_findings.title: Installer Runs System Package and Python Package Commands
medium_findings.description: The installer updates apt indexes, installs system packages, upgrades pip, and installs requirements. This is expected for audio rendering, but it changes the host environment and retrieves packages from configured repositories.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and not obfuscated, and they match the skill purpose. Risk remains because package manager execution changes the system and depends on external package sources.

medium_findings.title: Documented Local Subprocess Rendering
medium_findings.description: The skill documentation tells agents to call render_electronic.py through subprocess.run. The arguments are passed as a list and target a local script, but this still executes a child process during use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed examples do not use shell=True or concatenate shell strings, which lowers command injection concern. Confidence is high that execution exists, but medium that it is only a controlled local rendering workflow.

medium_findings.title: Local File Writes and Temporary File Cleanup
medium_findings.description: The helper scripts create output directories, write MIDI, JSON, WAV, and MP3 files, and remove temporary MIDI and WAV files. This is normal for media generation, but callers should choose output paths carefully.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code performs clear local output operations, not hidden file access. Risk is moderate because CLI-provided paths can write files wherever the running user has permission.

low_findings.title: Static High-Severity Token Matches Were Mostly Music Terminology
low_findings.description: Several scanner hits for weak cryptography, key files, and C2 keywords map to music concepts such as key_signature, MIDI files, genre descriptions, and private skill paths. No evidence found of credential collection, cryptography, or command-and-control behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines show music metadata, preset names, and file locations rather than sensitive secrets. Confidence is high, though not absolute because only context around flagged areas was reviewed.

low_findings.title: Documentation Links Are External but Not Runtime Exfiltration
low_findings.description: The skill includes external documentation links for music21, General MIDI, music theory, and IMSLP. These are user-facing references, not code that sends local data over the network.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are Markdown resource links, and no requests or urllib runtime call was found in the reviewed code. This supports a false-positive assessment for network exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Package Manager Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The installer executes apt-get and pip commands. This should be disclosed because it changes the local environment and may access package repositories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Direct package manager commands are present in the installer. They are legitimate setup steps but remain a sensitive execution pattern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Child Process Invocation in Usage Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation uses subprocess.run to invoke the electronic renderer. The reviewed calls use argv lists and local script paths.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is clearly present, but its context is controlled local rendering. No shell string injection evidence was found.

content.value_statement: Creating finished audio from text prompts requires composition logic, MIDI handling, and rendering tools. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured music creation and MP3 export.
actual_capabilities: Guides custom instrumental composition with music21 patterns.
Renders MIDI compositions to WAV and MP3 with FluidSynth.
Provides an electronic rendering pipeline for house, techno, trance, ambient, and acid house styles.
Analyzes MIDI files into structured JSON with tempo, key, tracks, and notes.
Transforms MIDI-derived JSON by transposing, changing tempo, looping sections, and swapping instruments.
Validates rendered audio for clipping, silence, duration, loudness, and dynamic range.
limitations: It does not generate vocals or lyrics as sung audio.
Rendered sound quality depends on installed packages, SoundFonts, and synthesis presets.
It requires local Python, FFmpeg, FluidSynth, and package dependencies for full rendering.
It is not a professional mixing, mastering, or real-time playback environment.

use_cases.title: Score Music for Short Videos
use_cases.description: Create timed instrumental MP3 tracks with requested mood, length, and instrumentation.

use_cases.title: Prototype Game or App Audio
use_cases.description: Generate loops, stingers, and background themes that can be exported as audio files.

use_cases.title: Study and Rework MIDI Pieces
use_cases.description: Inspect MIDI structure, change instruments or tempo, and render alternate arrangements.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Theme
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a short composition.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 30-second instrumental theme in C major for piano and strings. Export it as an MP3 and check for clipping.

prompt_templates.title: Match a Video Duration
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with timing and mood constraints.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compose a 75-second hopeful background track for a product video. Use piano, cello, and light percussion, then render an MP3.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Electronic Track
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request using the electronic renderer.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a deep house track with drums, sub bass, pads, and a sparse lead. Use the electronic pipeline and export a 192 kbps MP3.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze and Transform MIDI
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for MIDI inspection and arrangement changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this MIDI file, summarize its tempo and tracks, transpose it up two semitones, change piano to violin, and render the result.

output_examples.input: Create a 45-second calm piano and cello cue for a meditation intro.
output_examples.output: A rendered MP3 with a slow tempo, soft piano arpeggios, sustained cello notes, and a validation summary showing no clipping.

output_examples.input: Make a one-minute techno loop with strong drums and a synth lead.
output_examples.output: A downloadable MP3 rendered with the techno preset, plus a short note describing tempo, structure, and any audio warnings.

output_examples.input: Turn this classical MIDI into a flute and harp arrangement.
output_examples.output: A transformed arrangement rendered as MP3, with a brief summary of detected tracks, instrument changes, and duration.

best_practices: Specify duration, mood, tempo, and target instruments before rendering.
Render to a dedicated outputs directory and validate the final audio file.
Use the electronic pipeline for EDM styles and the traditional pipeline for acoustic instruments.
anti_patterns: Do not request vocals or sung lyrics as final audio.
Do not run the installer without understanding that it uses apt and pip.
Do not overwrite important files with output paths from experiments.

faq.question: Can this skill create downloadable audio files?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed to render instrumental compositions to MP3 or WAV files.

faq.question: Does it support electronic music?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a synthesis renderer with presets for deep house, techno, trance, ambient, acid house, and default styles.

faq.question: Can it analyze an existing MIDI file?
faq.answer: Yes. The MIDI inventory script extracts tempo, key, tracks, instruments, and note timing into JSON.

faq.question: Does it need system dependencies?
faq.answer: Yes. Full rendering needs Python packages, FFmpeg, FluidSynth, and SoundFonts for the traditional pipeline.

faq.question: Can it make vocals?
faq.answer: No. The skill focuses on instrumental MIDI and synthesized audio, not sung vocal generation.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with warnings. Review found no malicious intent, but the installer and rendering scripts execute commands and write files.
